chapters.title: Shattered Ink
chapters.summary: The paradox crystal shatters, turning every object into a living word. The Echoed Gaze awakens, the Hall of Mirrors collapses, and Elisa trades a vivid memory of her mother for a glyph that steadies the lattice. The agents perform a meta‑binding rite, each scarred by a personal regret, sealing the collapse. The new Glass of Memory glows, but a pulsing fissure in the lattice hints at a more volatile next cycle.
unresolved_threads: What exact price will the sentient Glass of Memory demand from the agents in the next cycle?
How will the pulsing fissure in the lattice affect the world’s stability as the new cycle begins?
arc_targets: Paradox crystal shatter triggers narrative recursion

Collapse of Hall of Mirrors is cemented

Price of the Glass of Memory.
What exact price will the sentient Glass of Memory demand from the agents in the next cycle?
How will the pulsing fissure in the lattice affect the world’s stability as the new cycle begins?

must_fix: Show collapse tightening in real time—words solidifying, breath turning to ink, agents feeling the threat; replace generic lullaby with a vivid, sensory memory that physically tears from Elisa’s mind; reduce placeholder tags to only critical meta‑aware moments; give each supporting agent a distinct, costly reaction during the binding rite; integrate the Hall of Mirrors collapse into a single, vivid image and record it as a collapsed artifact.

events: Paradox crystal shatters – living manuscript emerges.
Meta‑binding rite completed – authorial voices sacrificed.
Glass of Memory gains new glyphs – flag next_cycle_ready set.
